mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending expectations and forces corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds the 5.0 percent threshold over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based demand contraction that directly reduces corporate revenue growth and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance, yielding a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals resilient output growth that offsets higher discount rates, driving short-term equity momentum and positive forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during tightening periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5 for the positive return hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 195.0 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, invalidating the tightening expectation channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment indicates a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer demand, supporting corporate revenues and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over rolling windows, or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with improved corporate profit margins and upward earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns, with a p-value exceeding 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side weakness that triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances when INDPRO crosses below its trend, rejecting the risk-off positioning mechanism.
